claims.claim: For cylindrical-type battery cells, second venting accompanied by thermal runaway was observed at 24.2 s after the first venting was confirmed.
claims.quote: In the case of cylindrical- and pouch-type battery cells, second venting accompanied by thermal runaway was observed at 24.2 s and 67 s, respectively, after the first venting was confirmed

claims.claim: For pouch-type battery cells, second venting accompanied by thermal runaway was observed at 67 s after the first venting was confirmed.
claims.quote: In the case of cylindrical- and pouch-type battery cells, second venting accompanied by thermal runaway was observed at 24.2 s and 67 s, respectively, after the first venting was confirmed

claims.claim: With cooling fan operation, DMC gas detection time at the suction pipe was 0.558 s, compared to 2.968 s without fan operation, representing an 81.18% reduction in detection time.
claims.quote: The detection times of DMC gas at the suction pipe were 2.968 s and 0.558 s, respectively, indicating a significant difference of 81.18 %. This difference is due to the forced convection effect of the cooling fan, which increased the advection rate of DMC gas toward the suction pipe.

claims.claim: During the early stage of first venting, the cooling fan did not significantly affect gas flow behavior because the venting momentum was significantly larger than the flow field induced by the cooling fan.
claims.quote: operating the cooling fan rarely affects DMC gas flows during the early venting stage because the venting momentum is significantly larger than the flow field induced by the cooling fan.

claims.claim: Increasing the cooling fan intensity by 30% yielded a 22.28% improvement in detection time for left-side venting and a 19.57% improvement for right-side venting when venting occurred in the last battery cell.
claims.quote: Further increasing the fan intensity by 30 % yielded a 22.28 % improvement for left-side venting and a 19.57 % improvement for right-side venting.

claims.claim: The detection time difference between left-side and right-side venting was greatest for the middle battery cell, reaching 4.021 × 10−1 s, and smallest for the last battery cell.
claims.quote: The time difference was most notable in the middle cells, depending on the venting direction, and reached 4.021 × 10−1 s. In contrast, the difference at the last battery cell was the smallest.

claims.claim: When the cooling fan operated at 30% increased intensity (6.635 × 10−3 kg/s), detection was achieved within 1000 s across all venting locations in the last battery cell cases, and the detection time difference between left- and right-side venting increased with fan intensity.
claims.quote: when the fan operated at a 30 % increased intensity, corresponding to 6.635 × 10−3 kg/s, venting was detected within 1000 s in all cases. It was suggested that the optimal detection performance could be achieved under the current sensor configuration when the cooling fan operated at a higher intensity, and the detection time difference between left- and right-side venting increased with the fan intensity.

claims.claim: Nano SiAG felt achieved the highest temperature difference (ΔTTIM 1) of 578°C between the triggered cell and the adjacent cell, indicating the strongest thermal runaway propagation mitigation among the tested materials.
claims.quote: the ΔTTIM 1 values for each experimental group are as follows: Nano SiAG felt group (578 °C), mica plate group (541 °C), SiAG pre-oxidized fiber group (523 °C), aluminosilicate ceramic fiber group (−152 °C), and the group without thermal insulation material (−767 °C).

claims.claim: Aluminosilicate ceramic fiber insulation did not prevent thermal runaway propagation to all cells, but significantly reduced the propagation rate, extending jet fire time intervals from 53 s, 30 s, 15 s (no insulation) to 130 s, 120 s, 37 s respectively.
claims.quote: in the group without insulation material, the time intervals for jet fire of Cells 2–4 were 53 s, 30 s, and 15 s, respectively, while in the experimental group using aluminosilicate ceramic fiber insulation material, the corresponding time intervals were extended to 130 s, 120 s, and 37 s.

claims.claim: Aluminosilicate ceramic fiber exhibited the highest mass loss among TIMs, reaching 33.18% at 900°C with a continuous non-stepped weight loss profile, indicative of poor thermal stability in high-temperature environments.
claims.quote: aluminosilicate ceramic fiber exhibited a continuous, non-stepped weight loss profile, reaching a maximum weight loss of 33.18 % at 900 °C, indicative of poor thermal stability in high-temperature environments.

claims.claim: The elevated NO2 peak observed specifically in the mica plate group is attributed to the oxidative thermal decomposition of the nitrogen-based adhesive in the mica binder, not to electrochemical reactions within the cell.
claims.quote: the significantly elevated NO2 peak observed specifically in the Mica plate group is attributed to the oxidative thermal decomposition of the nitrogen-based adhesive used in the mica binder, rather than electrochemical reactions within the cell.

claims.claim: Aluminosilicate ceramic fiber experienced the most significant structural shrinkage after TRP testing, with a 69% contour area shrinkage rate, which led to thermal insulation failure.
claims.quote: the most significant lateral shrinkage and uneven longitudinal change for the aluminosilicate ceramic fiber group (69 % contour area shrinkage rate). The significant shrinkage of the aluminosilicate ceramic fiber group led to thermal insulation failure.

claims.claim: Nano SiAG felt exhibited minimal final mass loss of only 2.51% at 900°C in TGA tests, suggesting good thermal stability for medium-to-high temperature insulation applications.
claims.quote: Nano SiAG felt also exhibited minimal final weight loss, with a reduction of only 2.51 % at 900 °C, suggesting that it performs well as an insulating material in medium-to high-temperature ranges.

claims.claim: A cell with higher internal resistance, when subjected to an internal short circuit, generates more heat via Joule heating, shortens thermal runaway incubation time, and increases the risk of propagation to adjacent cells.
claims.quote: in the event of an internal short circuit (ISC), the large current (I) flowing through a higher internal resistance (R) generates a significantly larger and more rapid burst of heat... this intense initial heat generation acts as a more potent trigger, drastically shortening the incubation time for thermal runaway and accelerating the onset of subsequent exothermic chain reactions... a cell with higher internal resistance is not only more prone to initiating TR but also transfers heat more violently to adjacent cells, thereby increasing the risk of propagation.

claims.claim: For both NCA and LFP batteries, as SOC decreases, the UFL of the BVG remains nearly constant while the LFL increases sharply, causing the UFL and LFL curves to form a peninsula shape and converge to equal values at a critical low SOC.
claims.quote: For both types of batteries, the UFL remained nearly constant as the SOC decreased, but the LFL kept nearly constant ﬁrst and then increased sharply. The UFL and LFL approached the same value as the SOC decreases. The curves of ﬂammability limits of the BVG had a phenomenon of ﬂammability peninsula with decreasing SOC.

claims.claim: At the same SOC, the LFP battery BVG has a higher LFL and lower flammability range (RF) than the NCA battery BVG, resulting in a significantly lower flammability hazard index (HF) for LFP.
claims.quote: Compared with the NCA battery, the LFL of the LFP battery's vent gas is higher, and the RF is lower, resulting in lower HF signiﬁcantly at the same SOC. This is one of the main reasons for LFP batteries being safer than other variants.

claims.claim: For a SOC range of 0% to 130%, the maximum HF of the LFP battery BVG is 381% and that of the NCA battery BVG is 519%, with LFP being 27% lower than NCA.
claims.quote: At a SOC of 0%–130%, the maximum HF of the LFP and NCA batteries' vent gas is 381% and 519%, respectively. The former is 27% lower than the latter.

claims.claim: Increasing the CO2 diluent ratio (DR) at constant SOC causes the RF of the BVG to decrease significantly; for LFP LIBs the maximum RF dropped by 8%, 20%, 35%, and 49% at DR of 1, 2, 3, and 4 respectively compared with no dilution, and for NCA LIBs it dropped by 15%, 35%, 60%, and 87% at the same DR values.
claims.quote: It dropped by 8%, 20%, 35% and 49% for the LFP LIB, and by 15%, 35%, 60% and 87% for the NCA LIB, when the DR was 1, 2, 3 and 4 compared with no dilution, respectively.

claims.claim: For a SOC range of 0% to 130%, the maximum diluent ratio threshold (DRF) required to render the BVG non-flammable is 3.4 for LFP LIBs and 4.2 for NCA LIBs, with LFP being 19% lower than NCA.
claims.quote: For a SOC range of 0% to 130%, the maximum DRFs were 3.4 and 4.2 for LFP and NCA LIBs, respectively. The former is 19% lower than the latter.
